description: Fantasy requires element configurations that establish a world with internally consistent supernatural rules, meaningful magical objects, and distinct realms that differ from mundane reality.

character_constraints.description: A character who navigates the fantasy world — may be native or a newcomer from the ordinary world.
A wise figure who understands the world's magical rules and provides guidance.
An opposing force that uses or represents the dark side of the world's supernatural system.

relationship_constraints.description: The protagonist needs someone to explain the rules of magic and the world.

place_constraints.description: A world governed by supernatural rules — the primary arena of the story.
A mundane starting world that contrasts with the fantasy realm (portal fantasy) or the pre-adventure state.
The seat of dark power that the protagonist must ultimately confront.

object_constraints.description: At least one magical object that operates within the world's supernatural system — must have defined rules and costs.
A weapon with magical properties — its power must have limitations.

element_rules.description: Magic must have rules and costs — no unlimited power without consequence.
element_rules.testable_condition: Every magical object has a non-empty 'rules' array that includes at least one limitation or cost.

element_rules.description: The fantasy world must have at least one place with explicit supernatural rules that differ from mundane reality.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one place has a non-empty 'rules' array describing supernatural mechanics.

element_rules.description: The supernatural system must be internally consistent — rules established early must hold throughout.
element_rules.testable_condition: No timeline transition violates a rule established in an object or place's 'rules' array.
